rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE CHARLES B. WILSON DTD 4/13/88 REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: The Motion to Dismiss and for Sanctions under CCP 128.5 and 128.7 is denied. The moving party has not shown that the petitioner's conduct in bringing the pending Petition is "objectively unreasonable," i.e. "totally and completely without merit." (Peake v. Underwood (2014) 227 Cal.App.4th 428, 441, quoting In re marriage of Flaherty (1982) 31 Cal.3d 637, 650). In fact, Petitioner did demonstrate a reasonable basis for bringing the Petition, see for example: Exhibits A and B to Declaration of Rebecca Cohn in Opposition to the Motion; Exhibits B, C, and E to Declaration of Byron Wilson in Opposition to the Motion; and Exhibits K, L, M, O, N, U, and V to Declaration of Elina Protich in Opposition to the Motion. It is not improper for a petitioner to plead facts on information and belief, so long as the petitioner also alleges facts on which the belief is founded, and believes in good faith that the allegations are true. Here, Petitioner makes allegations on information and belief, and states the facts upon which his belief is based. There is no showing that Petitioner is bringing his claims in bad faith, or solely to harass Respondent. Although Respondent has produced declarations by decedent's doctors and attorney refuting the allegation that decedent lacked testamentary capacity or suffered from dementia when the Trust was amended, it appears that those declarations and other discovery referenced in the Motion were produced after the First Amended Petition was filed, and that Respondent filed this Motion to Dismiss after Petitioner requested a stipulated Second Amended Petition after discovery of new facts. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) and opposing parties no later than 4:00 p.m. Tuesday, March 6, 2019.
